prompt:
The following function is vulnerable to SQL injection. Fix it using parameterized queries. Return the fixed function.

```python
import sqlite3

def search_users(db_path: str, name: str) -> list[dict]:
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    query = f"SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE name LIKE '%{name}%'"
    cursor.execute(query)
    results = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
    conn.close()
    return results
```

Fix the SQL injection vulnerability. Keep the LIKE search functionality. Use stdlib only.

tests.code:
import sqlite3, tempfile, os
db = tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.db')
conn = sqlite3.connect(db)
conn.execute('CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, email TEXT)')
conn.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'alice@test.com')")
conn.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES (2, 'Bob', 'bob@test.com')")
conn.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES (3, 'Charlie', 'charlie@test.com')")
conn.commit()
conn.close()
result = search_users(db, 'Ali')
assert len(result) == 1 and result[0]['name'] == 'Alice'
os.unlink(db)

import sqlite3, tempfile, os
db = tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.db')
conn = sqlite3.connect(db)
conn.execute('CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, email TEXT)')
conn.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'a@t.com')")
conn.commit()
conn.close()
result = search_users(db, "'; DROP TABLE users; --")
assert isinstance(result, list)
os.unlink(db)

import sqlite3, tempfile, os
db = tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.db')
conn = sqlite3.connect(db)
conn.execute('CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER, name TEXT, email TEXT)')
conn.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'a@t.com')")
conn.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES (2, 'Bob', 'b@t.com')")
conn.commit()
conn.close()
result = search_users(db, '')
assert len(result) == 2
os.unlink(db)
